name: Arnedillo and Ajedrezado Caves Loop
name_original: Via Verde Cidacos por Arnedillo — circular por las Cuevas del Ajedrezado
description: Hike the Via Verde Cidacos por Arnedillo, a moderate 9.3-mile circular route exploring a greenway and the historical Ajedrezado Caves.
region_intro: Hiking around Santa Eulalia Bajera offers diverse landscapes within the Cidacos Valley, characterized by distinctive clay-colored cliffs and lush riverbanks. The region is framed by the mountainous Sierra de la Hez and Sierra de Peñalmonte, providing varied terrain for exploration. Hikers can encounter impressive rock formations and historical sites, including ancient castles and hermitages. This area provides a rich environment for outdoor activities, blending natural beauty with cultural interest. Best hiking trails around Santa Eulalia Bajera The most popular hiking route is Arnedillo and Ajedrezado Caves Loop, a 9.3 miles (14.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 15 minutes to complete. This route explores the Cidacos Valley and passes by the Ajedrezado Caves. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peña del Castillo and Cidacos Greenway Loop, a moderate 4.2 miles (6.8 km) path. This trail offers views of Peña del Castillo and follows sections of the Cidacos Greenway. Local hikers also love the Herce Castle and El Salvador Hermitage Trail, a 2.6 miles (4.2 km) trail leading through historical landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 15 minutes. Hiking around Santa Eulalia Bajera is defined by the Cidacos Valley, clay-colored cliffs, and historical sites like castles and hermitages. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to moderate ascents. The routes in Santa Eulalia Bajera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Santa Eulalia Bajera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Santa Eulalia Bajera?
A: There are over 40 hiking routes around Santa Eulalia Bajera, offering a variety of experiences. These include 14 easy trails, 30 moderate options, and a few more challenging routes for experienced hikers.

Q: What types of landscapes and natural features can I expect to see while hiking?
A: The region is defined by the spectacular &lt;strong&gt;Cidacos Valley&lt;/strong&gt;, featuring distinctive clay-colored cliffs and lush green river banks. You'll also encounter impressive rock formations, particularly around Arnedillo, and the mountainous backdrop of the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de la Hez&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Peñalmonte&lt;/strong&gt;. A small waterfall near Préjano adds to the natural beauty.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes offer circular paths. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199657133/pena-del-castillo-and-cidacos-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Castillo and Cidacos Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.2-mile circular trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1200929367/mirador-de-san-marcos-circular-desde-arnedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de San Marcos — circular desde Arnedo&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views.

Q: What historical sites or attractions can I visit along the trails?
A: Hikers can explore several historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1200821839/herce-castle-and-el-salvador-hermitage-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herce Castle and El Salvador Hermitage Trail&lt;/a&gt; leads through historical landscapes. You can also find the remains of the 15th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5344963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Préjano Castle&lt;/a&gt;, with its 15-meter high tower, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6307877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas del Ajedrezado of Santa Eulalia Somera&lt;/a&gt; along the Via Verde Cidacos.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options?
A: Yes, there are 14 easy trails suitable for families or beginners. The Via Verde Cidacos, for instance, is a moderate 9.3-mile route that is generally suitable for various fitness levels, offering gentle inclines and descents through scenic greenways.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Santa Eulalia Bajera?
A: The trails in Santa Eulalia Bajera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 80 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the blend of natural beauty with historical sites, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various ability levels.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to viewpoints or offer panoramic vistas?
A: Yes, for panoramic views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1200929367/mirador-de-san-marcos-circular-desde-arnedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de San Marcos — circular desde Arnedo&lt;/a&gt;. This route offers excellent vantage points over the surrounding landscape.

Q: Can I find any natural thermal springs near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, a significant natural draw in the region is the presence of freely accessible thermal springs located on the riverbanks in Arnedillo. These offer a relaxing experience, perfect for unwinding after a hike.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes available?
A: For those seeking a more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1200958076/pena-isasa-loop-from-prejano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa Loop from Préjano&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route with a significant elevation gain of over 750 meters, offering a more strenuous trek.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Santa Eulalia Bajera?
A: While specific recommendations depend on personal preference, the diverse landscapes of Santa Eulalia Bajera, with its valleys and mountains, offer pleasant hiking conditions during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are still enjoyable. Winter offers a different, often quieter, experience.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by caves or unique geological formations?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1245354968/arnedillo-and-ajedrezado-caves-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnedillo and Ajedrezado Caves Loop&lt;/a&gt; explores the Cidacos Valley and passes by the fascinating &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6307877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuevas del Ajedrezado of Santa Eulalia Somera&lt;/a&gt;, offering a unique geological highlight.

Q: Are there any specific natural monuments to look out for?
A: Beyond the general landscape, you can find specific natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6307839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red Cliffs of Herce&lt;/a&gt;, which are notable geological features in the area.
tips: The Cidacos greenway offers you fantastic landscapes through a very smooth road with slight ups and downs. The Dinosaur Route is part of the MyMerak Route through the western area of La Rioja.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this hiking route?
faqs.answer: While the Vía Verde del Cidacos section is generally easy and suitable for various users, the addition of the circular route to the Ajedrezado Caves involves an ascent to an elevated position. This makes the overall difficulty of the hike moderate.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The route combines two distinct types of terrain. The main section follows the Vía Verde del Cidacos, which is built on an old railway line, featuring a generally flat surface of asphalt and compacted gravel. However, the circular part leading to the Ajedrezado Caves involves a more varied and ascending path.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in or around Arnedillo, which serves as a convenient starting point for this circular route. The Vía Verde del Cidacos is accessible from various points, but starting from Arnedillo allows for easy access to both the greenway and the ascent to the caves.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Vía Verde del Cidacos is generally dog-friendly. You can bring your dog along, but please keep them on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas or near wildlife habitats, and remember to clean up after them.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to hike this route are typically spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is usually mild and pleasant, making for comfortable hiking conditions. You'll also enjoy the vibrant natural scenery, from riverine forests to cultivated lands.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail or the caves?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or to visit the Ajedrezado Caves. The Vía Verde del Cidacos is a public path, and the caves are an open archaeological site.

faqs.question: What interesting sights or landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in points of interest. You'll follow the scenic Cidacos Greenway, pass by the Hermitage of San Tirso, and enjoy views from the Gurugus Viewpoint. Don't miss the hot spring pools, the Hermitage of San Andres and San Blas, and the historic Arnedillo Castle. The highlight is undoubtedly the unique Ajedrezado Caves with their distinctive chessboard pattern.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I observe on this hike?
faqs.answer: The rocky cliffs along the Cidacos River, especially around Arnedillo, are home to a significant colony of vultures and other rupicolous (rock-dwelling) birds. Keep an eye out for them, particularly from viewpoints. The riverine forests also support diverse birdlife.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations or considerations since this trail is within a Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is located within the Valles del Leza, Jubera, Cidacos y Alhama Biosphere Reserve. While there are no specific entrance fees, please adhere to general responsible tourism guidelines: stay on marked paths, do not disturb wildlife, take all your trash with you, and respect the natural and cultural heritage of the area, especially around the historical caves.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route primarily follows and incorporates sections of the Vía Verde del Cidacos. You'll also find it intersects with parts of the Camino Natural Via Verde del rio Cidacos and briefly with the Vía Verde de Préjano, as these are all part of the broader greenway network in the region.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or cafes along the trail?
faqs.answer: While the Vía Verde itself is a natural path, you'll find amenities like cafes, restaurants, and restrooms in the towns and villages along the route, particularly in Arnedillo and Santa Eulalia Somera. It's a good idea to plan your stops accordingly, especially before heading up to the Ajedrezado Caves.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of San Andres and San Blas
